content.zh:
Helicopter Cube Tutorial (3)

Yujian Song

Rotating regulation is the same as previous tutorials.

Cluster Method of the Helicopter Cube

We have known that the center blocks are combined by four clusters, and jumble rotations can exchange blocks of different clusters. In previous tutorials, to solve the Helicopter Cube, we do cubeshape first, then solve it layer by layer, which is easy to understand for beginners, but in this method, jumble rotations might be needed while solving any center block, so the actual operation may be very complicated. There is a more reasonable idea: To transform the Helicopter into the HeliDoctre, can we make center blocks be in correct clusters by using some jumble rotations after doing cubeshape?

This method is more effective than layer-by-layer method, and more suitable to apply to 12 axis diamond puzzles. In this tutorial, we are going to talk about the cluster method of Helicopter Cube.

We are going to use the formula below (abbreviated as #UF, which means the middle step is to rotate edge UF) to do exchanging between clusters.

UR+ FL+ UF UR- FL-

It is easy to see that, this formula exchanges the blue and white center blocks, but does not change their clusters, only the red and orange blocks being exchanged between different clusters. So we can use this formula to correct blocks between two clusters. Here is an example:

Do cubeshape first.

Because the four center blocks with the same color belong to four different clusters, we can adjust them in color order. For example, to get in four different clusters, the white blocks can be moved into the same face by the operation indicated by the arrows:

And in the picture below, the cyan blocks belong to the same cluster:

So doing the operation below can move one white block into its correct cluster:

......which means doing formula #UB. Similarly, doing #DB can move the other white block into its correct cluster.

Then solve the blocks with other colors. For example, solving the yellow blocks in the picture below, because doing #UF can exchange the yellow and green blocks, and we do not correct green blocks yet, we can do #UF directly.

Assume that we have corrected the white, yellow, red and orange blocks, like the picture above. It is easy to see that doing UB #UR can move all green blocks into their correct clusters, but it will exchange the green and orange blocks. Because the orange block has been in its correct cluster, we need to replace the orange block with the block not being correct cluster (the blue block in this example) first, then do the formula to correct the green block, which means doing UF UB #UR. At this time, all center blocks without blue blocks are in correct clusters, so blue blocks are in their own clusters naturally.

Then we can solve it as a HeliDoctre Cube. There might be a special situation below:

This is a pair-exchanging of center blocks, and to solve it, we need to exchange blocks with same color between different clusters. For the example above, do UB #UR to exchange them, then do UB UF UR UF UR UF to solve it.

The Way to Solve Curvy Copter

There are many kinds of derivations of the Helicopter Cube. The most similar puzzle with Helicopter is the Curvy Cube, which only has more edge blocks than Helicopter.

Curvy Copter, image from TwistyPuzzles

The way to solve Curvy Cube is same as Helicopter, being much easier by using cluster method. When solving it as a CurvyDortre Cube, we need to find the edge blocks and correct them directions. After correcting the directions of edge blocks on the bottom and middle layers, for the top layer, correct the center blocks like Helicopter first, then use the formula UL- FR- UF UL+ FR+ FL+ UR+ UF FL- UR- UF to correct the directions of edge blocks on the top layer:

UL- FR- UF UL+ FR+ FL+ UR+ UF FL- UR- UF

At last, using the 3-cycle formula of Helicopter to correct the positions of corner blocks. It is interesting that there will not be the corner pair-exchanging situation. The reason can be analyzed by using the knowledge about permutation parity.
